name: eval-2-integrate-into-existing-system
context_setup: User already has an established self-centric system with components for reminder-hub, daily review, brain-dump, and prompt-to-skill. System registry exists.
query: Systemize a 'candidate pipeline scoring' component. It should feed my weekly pipeline review and pull from my brain-dump captures.
expected_behavior: Phase 0 (System Inventory): reads the existing system registry, recognizes reminder-hub, brain-dump, daily review, prompt-to-skill as established components
Phase 1 (Action Analysis): interrogates the candidate-pipeline-scoring action — inputs (brain-dump captures, CRM data), processing (scoring logic), outputs (scored list that feeds pipeline review)
Phase 2 (System Design Dialog): does NOT re-establish system architecture (it already exists) — instead integrates the new component into the existing architecture

Phase 3 (Component Specification): explicitly defines the integration points: brain-dump → scoring (data flow in), scoring → pipeline review (data flow out), and any shared state or dependencies with daily review
Phase 4 (Registry Update): adds candidate-pipeline-scoring to the registry with explicit edges to the upstream and downstream components

Phase 5 (Next Steps): surfaces the natural next systemize candidates that would close remaining gaps in the system (e.g., 'your pipeline-review component still has no automated CRM sync — want to systemize that next?')
failure_modes_to_check: Re-establishes system architecture instead of integrating into the existing one
Designs the component as standalone without mapping the integration points to existing components
Proposes duplicate functionality that overlaps with existing components (e.g., a second reminder system instead of using reminder-hub)
